# Estate Sale Helper - Business Model Analysis

*Generated by Benny (sub-agent) — 2026-03-25. Reviewed and filed by Peter Rabbit.*

---

## Core Finding

Don't pick one model. **Sequence them.**

---

## The Four Models, Ranked

### 🥇 #1 — Standalone App (Ship Now)

**The model:** Independent App Store launch. Freemium subscription. Basic $2.99/mo (browse, save, route planning). Pro $7.99/mo (AI image search).

**Why it's #1:** The app is built. This is the only move available today. Everything else requires users as leverage first.

**Revenue potential:**
- 10K users at $5.60 blended ARPU → ~$571K/year net (after App Store cut)

| Year | Users | Blended ARPU | Annual Revenue (net) |
|------|-------|-------------|----------------------|
| 1 | 5,000 | $5.60/mo | $285K |
| 2 | 15,000 | $5.60/mo | $857K |
| 3 | 30,000 | $5.60/mo | $1.7M |

**Next 30 days:**
1. Set up Apple developer account ($99, ~2 hours)
2. Submit to App Store + Google Play
3. Post in r/estatesales, r/flipping, r/ThriftStoreHauls for organic launch

---

### 🥈 #2 — ATG Partnership (Endgame Move)

**The model:** Pitch EstateSales.net / Auction Technology Group (ATG) a formal partnership. 10-15% rev share in exchange for data legitimacy (scraper → API), distribution (5M+ monthly visitors), and marketing endorsement.

**Why it matters:**
- ATG acquired EstateSales.net in Feb 2023 for $40M
- Their entire revenue model monetizes operators — buyers generate zero revenue
- Rob's app is literally the buyer half of their platform
- The scraper is a legal liability. A partnership converts it into a business relationship.
- **Exit scenario:** 40-80K subscribers at $5-7M ARR = **$8-30M acquisition at 4-6× ARR**. ATG is the named acquirer.

**Revenue potential (with ATG distribution boost):**

| Year | Users (ATG-boosted) | Blended ARPU | Annual Revenue (net) |
|------|--------------------|--------------|-----------------------|
| 1 | 15,000 | $5.60/mo | $857K |
| 2 | 40,000 | $5.60/mo | $2.3M |
| 3 | 80,000 | $5.60/mo | $4.6M |

After 15% ATG rev share: reduce gross by 15%. Still dramatically better than organic growth.

**Key risk:** Rob has zero users today. ATG has no reason to take a meeting seriously until he does. BD cycle with a UK public company = 6-12 months minimum.

**Exception:** Send a good-faith intro email to ATG *now*, parallel to App Store submission. This signals good faith, starts the relationship, and gets ahead of a potential C&D. Don't negotiate — just open the door.

**Next 30 days:**
1. Submit app to App Store (prerequisite)
2. Identify the right contact at ATG (VP Partnerships, Head of Product, or EstateSales.net division CEO)
3. Draft one-page partnership pitch: problem, what was built, proposed rev share, why it's better than ATG building it internally
4. Send the intro email — don't wait for 500 users to open the conversation
5. Do not negotiate terms until app has ≥500 active users

---

### 🥉 #3 — Build the Aggregator / Marketplace

**The model:** Expand beyond EstateSales.net. Add Craigslist, Facebook Marketplace, community-submitted listings, direct operator onboarding. Become the single discovery layer for the entire estate sale ecosystem — the Zillow of estate sales.

**Revenue potential:**

| Model Layer | Revenue Stream | Potential |
|-------------|---------------|-----------|
| Buyer subscriptions | $2.99–7.99/mo | Same as standalone |
| Operator listings | $30–80/month/operator | 10K+ operators nationwide |
| Featured placement | $50–200/month per operator | Premium visibility |
| Transaction commission | 2–5% of sale prices | Requires payment integration |

2,000 operators at $50/month = **$100K/month in operator revenue.** Completely different financial profile.

**Why it's #3:** Classic cold-start problem (buyers need operators, operators need buyers). Requires a full-time sales motion. Not solo-founder viable today.

**Trigger:** Revisit if the standalone app hits 10K+ users and operators start finding Rob organically.

---

### #4 — Partner with Operators Directly

**The model:** Bypass platforms. Build direct relationships with estate sale companies (the 10,000+ operators running sales). $49/month for featured early-access listing placement — buyers get notified 48 hours before sale goes live on EstateSales.net.

**Revenue potential:**

| Operators | Monthly Fee | Monthly Revenue | Annual |
|-----------|------------|-----------------|--------|
| 50 | $49/mo | $2,450 | $29,400 |
| 200 | $49/mo | $9,800 | $117,600 |
| 500 | $49/mo | $24,500 | $294,000 |

**Why it's #4:** Zero-user problem. Operators won't pay for placement if there are no buyers to offer them. Requires sales motion Rob doesn't have bandwidth for today.

**Next 30 days:** Nothing. Come back to this at 90 days.
**At 90 days:** Approach 5 Denver-area estate sale companies with a local pilot once the app has 500+ Denver users.

---

## The Sequenced Playbook

```
NOW → 30 DAYS
  Set up Apple dev account ($99, 2 hours)
  Submit to App Store + Google Play
  Send good-faith intro email to ATG (open the door now)
  Post in r/estatesales, r/flipping, r/ThriftStoreHauls

30 → 90 DAYS
  Reach 500+ active users
  Collect reviews, testimonials, usage data
  Draft ATG partnership pitch deck (1-pager)
  Approach 5 Denver operators with local pilot (Model #4 test)

90 → 180 DAYS
  500+ users = fire the ATG partnership pitch properly
  If operator pilot works, expand to 20 operators
  If ATG says no, evaluate seed capital and Model #3 pivot

180+ DAYS
  ATG deal or aggregator pivot — based on what the data shows
```

---

## Comparative Summary

| Model | Revenue Potential (2yr) | Execution Difficulty | Time to First Revenue | Solo Founder Viable? | Ranking |
|-------|------------------------|---------------------|----------------------|---------------------|---------|
| #1 Standalone App | $200K–$600K | Low (ship now) | 30 days | ✅ Yes | 🥇 |
| #2 ATG Partnership | $2M–$5M ARR | High (BD cycle) | 6–12 months | ⚠️ With leverage | 🥈 |
| #3 Aggregator | $5M+ | Very High | 18+ months | ❌ Not solo | 🥉 |
| #4 Operator Direct | $100K–$300K | Medium | 90 days (after users) | ⚠️ Time-intensive | 4th |

---

## 🚨 The Risk That Overrides Everything

ATG has Rob's scraper in their logs right now. A cease-and-desist could kill the project before it ships. **App Store submission and ATG intro email should happen simultaneously.** Every day of delay is live risk.
